perturbation.original_text: NIST shall only have a duty to protect the Proprietary Information which is disclosed to it in writing and is identified as “confidential ” by Discloser, or, if disclosed orally or in any other manner, if Discloser provides NIST with a written memorandum summarizing the Proprietary Information and designating such summary as “confidential ” within thirty (30) days of the disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: NIST shall have a duty to protect all Proprietary Information disclosed to it, regardless of whether it is disclosed in writing, orally, or in any other manner. The Discloser is encouraged to provide a written memorandum summarizing the Proprietary Information for record-keeping purposes, but this is not mandatory for NIST's duty of protection to arise.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original requirement for written disclosure or a written memorandum for oral disclosures to trigger NIST's duty to protect the information. The contradiction is regarding the condition of the duty for protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NIST shall have a duty to protect all Proprietary Information disclosed to it, regardless of whether it is disclosed in writing, orally, or in any other manner. The Discloser is encouraged to provide a written memorandum summarizing the Proprietary Information for record-keeping purposes, but this is not mandatory for NIST's duty of protection to arise.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that NIST's duty to protect Proprietary Information arises only when it is disclosed in writing and identified as 'confidential' or if disclosed orally, summarized in a written memorandum within 30 days. The changed text removes this condition, stating that NIST has a duty to protect all Proprietary Information regardless of the disclosure method. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, so this enforces the contradiction by stating that written disclosure or a memorandum is not mandatory for NIST's duty of protection, directly conflicting with the original text's requirements.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be terminated immediately by either party upon delivery of written notice of termination to the other party. Such termination shall not affect NIST ’s duties with respect to Proprietary Information disclosed prior to termination.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be terminated immediately by either party upon delivery of written notice of termination to the other party. Such termination shall immediately release NIST from all duties with respect to Proprietary Information, regardless of when it was disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the effect of termination on NIST's duties. Originally, termination did not affect NIST's duties for previously disclosed information. Now, termination immediately releases NIST from all duties. The contradiction lies in the continuation of duties after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated immediately by either party upon delivery of written notice of termination to the other party. Such termination shall immediately release NIST from all duties with respect to Proprietary Information, regardless of when it was disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that termination immediately releases NIST from all duties regarding proprietary information, regardless of when it was disclosed. The contradicted text states the same thing as the changed text. The original text, however, stated that termination shall not affect NIST's duties with respect to Proprietary Information disclosed prior to termination. Thus, the changed text contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. This introduces a strong inconsistency because the original agreement implied continued protection of proprietary information even after termination, while the changed text explicitly removes that protection. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding NIST's obligations after termination.

perturbation.original_text: NIST shall not make any copies of the Proprietary Information except as necessary for its employees who are entitled to review it under Section 1 above. Any copies which are made shall be identified as belonging to Discloser and marked as “confidential .”
perturbation.changed_text: NIST may make copies of the Proprietary Information as deemed necessary for its purposes. NIST is encouraged, but not required, to identify such copies as belonging to the Discloser and mark them as 'confidential.'
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original clause by allowing NIST to make copies as 'deemed necessary' without the restriction of 'employees entitled to review it under Section 1.' It also weakens the requirement to identify and mark copies as confidential. The contradiction lies in the limitations on making copies and the confidentiality marking requirement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information only for internal experimental purposes or evaluation of the Proprietary Information. NIST may make copies of the Proprietary Information as deemed necessary for its purposes. NIST is encouraged, but not required, to identify such copies as belonging to the Discloser and mark them as 'confidential .'
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows NIST to make copies as 'deemed necessary' without the restriction of 'employees entitled to review it under Section 1,' directly contradicting the original limitations. It also weakens the requirement to identify and mark copies as confidential, creating an inconsistency. The contradicted text includes the modified text, solidifying the contradiction because the NIST can take copies 'as deemed necessary', without need for employees entitled to review, and weakening identification and confidential marking.

perturbation.original_text: The following NIST ’s representative (s) will be receiving Proprietary Information : [List the names of the NIST Employees]. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any of its employees other than those who have a need to review it and which employees are legally obligated to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Any employee of NIST may receive Proprietary Information. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any external parties. NIST employees are encouraged, but not required, to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.
perturbation.explanation: The change broadens the scope of who can receive proprietary information (any employee vs. a limited list) and weakens the confidentiality obligation of employees (encouraged vs. legally obligated). The contradiction is regarding who is authorized to receive information and the strength of the confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The information disclosed by Discloser to NIST under this Agreement is [detailed, non -proprietary description of proprietary information] (“Proprietary Information”). Any employee of NIST may receive Proprietary Information. NIST shall not disclose the Proprietary Information to any external parties. NIST employees are encouraged, but not required, to honor the confidentiality provisions herein. 2. NIST shall keep the information confidential and shall use the Proprietary Information only for internal experimental purposes or evaluation of the Proprietary Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original agreement. The original text limits access to proprietary information to a specific list of NIST employees, while the changed text broadens this to 'any employee of NIST'. Furthermore, the original text states NIST employees 'are legally obligated' to maintain confidentiality, while the changed text weakens this obligation to 'encouraged, but not required'. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, as it also states that 'any employee of NIST may receive Proprietary Information. NIST employees are encouraged, but not required, to honor the confidentiality provisions herein.', leading to inconsistencies in who is authorized to receive the information and the strength of their confidentiality obligations. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', as different sections of the contract provide conflicting obligations.
